title: ulistget
text: ulistget Get an item from an item list. Explain this code long ulistget (UHACThList, intoption, intindex, UCHAR*Id, UCHAR**item); Copy Parameters hList Handle to the list, as returned by ulistnew option Ttype of item to be retrieved; possible values are: UITEM_OPTION_NONE —gets an item by index. You must supply a value for index ; the value for Id is ignored. UITEM_OPTION_ID —gets an item by ID. You must supply a value for Id ; the value for index is ignored. UITEM_OPTION_ID_CASE —as UITEM_OPTION_ID , except that the Id is handled in a case-sensitive manner (for example, getting an item with Id " a " does not get an item with Id " A "). index Index of the item, if the item is indexed Id ID of the item, if the item is associative item Contents of the item (Output) Return Values Table 1. Values returned by ulistget Value Meaning 0 Success. -165 The supplied handle hList is not valid. -1112 The value of option is not valid. -1203 The value of id is out of range. -1406 Memory could not be allocated or freed. Use Library: ulib Description ulistget gets an item from the specified list. This function is very similar to the ProcScript command getitem . Because this function returns a pointer by reference, you must free the allocated data after use by calling unifree . The variable option determines if the item is indexed or associative. Note that there is no safeguard if you do not provide the expected parameters. For example, if you use UITEM_OPTION_NONE , but fail to supply a value for Id , your program is likely to crash. It is allowed, although not recommended, to mix indexed and associative items in one list. For example, you can get an item by its index from a list that contains only associative items (but not the other way around). Example: ulistget and uuf2list The following example shows how to retrieve and process a list that was returned as an OUT parameter by a 4GL operation. The operation GETLIST in service TESTSRV is defined as follows: Explain this code operation getlist params string thelist : out; endparams putitem thelist, -1, "One" putitem thelist, -1, "Two" putitem thelist, -1, "Three" end Copy The following 3GL code activates service GETLIST, retrieves the indexed list, and loops through the items to print them out. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> #include <stdio.h> void get_itemlist () { UHACT hEnv, hIn; /* Environment and instance handle */ UHACT hOp; /* Operation handles for OP1 amd OP2 */ long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */ UHACT hList; /* Item list handle */ int i; /* Loop variable */ UCHAR *pItem; ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */ /* Instantiate service TESTSVC */ uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"TESTSVC", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr); /* Instantiate operation GETLIST */ uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"GETLIST", 0, &hOp); uopract(hOp, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Activate the operation */ ulistnew(hEnv, &hList); /* Create an item list */ uuf2list(hOp, 1, hList); /* Get the list data */ i=1; /* Loop through list items */ while ( ulistget(hList, UITEM_OPTION_NONE, i, 0, &pItem) == UACT_SUCCESS ) { printf("Item %d = '%s'\n", i, pItem); /* Print out item */ unifree(hList, pItem); /* Free item data */ i++; } uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */ ufreeh (&hList); /* Free list handle */ ufreeh (&hOp); /* Free operation handle */ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */ } Copy This code prints out: Explain this code Item 1 = 'One' Item 2 = 'Two' Item 3 = 'Three' Copy Related Links ulistnew Create an item list. uuf2list Copy list data from a basic parameter or entity field. getitem Copy an item from a list to a field or variable.
